member.name: Olivia Bailey
member.role: MP for Reading West and Mid Berkshire
member.synopsis: Olivia Bailey is the Labour MP for Reading West and Mid Berkshire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently holds the Government post of Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Department for Education) (Equalities).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Business: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to improve the regulatory environment for businesses in Reading West and Mid Berkshire constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 17 March 2025 this Government published the Action Plan for Regulation, outlining reforms to streamline regulation and support growth. As part of this we are cutting regulatory administrative costs to business by 25%, strengthening accountability for r...

writtenQuestions.heading: Dementia: Reading West and Mid Berkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, pursuant to the Answers of 11 November 2024 to Questions 12677, 12678 and 12679 on Dementia: Reading West and Mid Berkshire, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of collecting data o
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local systems rarely hold data by Parliamentary constituency. However, NHS England collects data on the number of people referred for assessments for dementia. This includes data about people diagnosed with dementia, including those who receive medication...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Conversion Therapy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what steps she is taking to ban conversion practices.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Conversion practices are abuse - they have no place in society and must be stopped.My ministerial colleagues and I are working hard to bring forward legislation to deliver a full trans-inclusive ban that offers protection from these abusive practices whil...

writtenQuestions.heading: Dementia: Reading West and Mid Berkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the average waiting time for a dementia assessment is in Reading West and Mid Berkshire constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England does not hold this information centrally. Lord Darzi’s independent review showed that a timely diagnosis is vital to ensuring that a person with dementia can access the advice, information, care, and support that can help them to live well and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dementia: Reading West and Mid Berkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many people are waiting for an appointment with a Memory Assessment Service for a dementia assessment in Reading West and Mid Berkshire constituency as of 4 November 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England does not hold this information centrally. Lord Darzi’s independent review showed that a timely diagnosis is vital to ensuring that a person with dementia can access the advice, information, care, and support that can help them to live well and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dementia: Reading West and Mid Berkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what proportion of people living with dementia in Reading West and Mid Berkshire constituency received a (a) care plan review and (b) new care plan in financial year 2023/24.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England does not hold this information centrally. NHS England is committed to delivering high quality care and support for every person with dementia, and central to this is the provision of personalised care and support planning for post diagnostic s...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Sterilisation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many people requested (a) vasectomies and (b) tubal ligations in the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This data is not held in the format requested.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Sterilisation: Costs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the average cost was of (a) vasectomies and (b) tubal ligations for (i) day cases and (ii) elective inpatients in the latest period for which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold data in the requested format. Through the 2022 Health and Care Act, the 2023/25 NHS Payment Scheme has replaced the National Tariff Payment System. This is a set of rules, prices and guidance used by commissioners and provider...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Sterilisation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many (a) vasectomy reversals and (b) female sterilisation reversals were (i) requested and (ii) performed in the last five years.
